mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals the onset of a monetary easing cycle, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% in August 2007 following August money-market stress
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive returns fail to materialize in >55% of easing-onset episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 15% year-over-year, it signals a broad-based residential investment contraction that historically precedes consumer demand weakness and credit tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1381 to 1354 thousand units vintage-to-revised in mid-2007, consistent with an ongoing housing downturn
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where the HOUST YoY decline trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that leads corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% in August 2007 with PAYEMS growth already decelerating
failure_condition: Hypothesis rejected if forward 60d returns following the UNRATE trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical trigger episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which is priced into equities with a short lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value of 114.4254 in August 2007 reflecting continued industrial expansion pre-crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, invalidating the short-horizon trend signal.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS declines while HOUST simultaneously falls sharply, the joint signal indicates the Fed is easing specifically in response to a housing-led credit contraction; this combination amplifies uncertainty about financial stability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, distinct from the standalone easing effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls month-over-month AND HOUST YoY decline exceeds 10%
evidence_refs: fact:August 2007 FEDFUNDS steady near 5.02% amid deepening housing downturn ahead of expected September rate cut
regime_hint: housing-led credit crisis with reactive monetary easing
failure_condition: If SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of joint-trigger episodes, or if the interaction effect adds no incremental Rank IC over the standalone FEDFUNDS signal, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion accompanied by an ongoing housing downturn, decelerating PAYEMS growth signals an approaching turning point in the business cycle that markets underprice, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this effect is absent in early or mid-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage near 138,037 thousand in August 2007 with growth slowing amid housing sector contraction
failure_condition: Hypothesis fails if the negative return relationship does not hold specifically in late-cycle housing-downturn regimes but instead appears uniformly across all cycle phases, or if Rank IC < 0.03 in regime-restricted samples.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS month-over-month growth turns negative for two consecutive months, it signals weakening consumer spending that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth negative for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 377,615 million vs revised 363,676 million in August 2007 indicating downward revision risk to consumer demand data
failure_condition: If forward 20d SPX returns following the RSAFS negative-growth trigger are positive in more than 55% of episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously near cycle highs, the joint condition signals a stagflation-like risk where the Fed cannot ease to support growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as markets price constrained policy flexibility.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3% AND FEDFUNDS above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 208.028 in July 2007 with FEDFUNDS at 5.02%, indicating limited policy easing room despite financial stress
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of joint-trigger episodes, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone, the hypothesis is rejected.
